rules.name: no-assigning-return-values
rules.description: disallow assigning return values of `cy` calls

messages.unexpected: Do not assign the return value of a Cypress command

rules.name: no-async-before
rules.description: disallow using `async`/`await` in Cypress `before` methods

rules.name: no-async-tests

messages.unexpected: Avoid using async functions with Cypress tests

rules.name: no-chained-get
rules.description: disallow chain of `cy.get()` calls

rules.name: no-debug
rules.description: disallow using `cy.debug()` calls

rules.name: no-force
rules.description: disallow using `force: true` with action commands

messages.unexpected: Do not use force on click and type calls

rules.name: no-pause
rules.description: disallow using `cy.pause()` calls

rules.name: no-unnecessary-waiting
rules.description: disallow waiting for arbitrary time periods

messages.unexpected: Do not wait for arbitrary time periods

rules.name: no-xpath
rules.description: disallow using `cy.xpath()` calls

messages.unexpected: cy.xpath() is deprecated and unsupported. Consider using cy.get() with appropriate selectors instead.

rules.name: require-data-selectors
rules.description: require `data-*` attribute selectors

messages.unexpected: use data-* attribute selectors instead of classes or tag names

rules.name: unsafe-to-chain-command
rules.description: disallow actions within chains

messages.unexpected: It is unsafe to chain further commands that rely on the subject after this command. It is best to split the chain, chaining again from `cy.` in a next command line.

rules.name: prefer-array-at
rules.description: Prefer Array.prototype.at() over length-based indexing

rules.name: prefer-array-fill
rules.description: Prefer Array.prototype.fill() over Array.from or map with constant values

messages.preferFillArrayFrom: Use Array.from({length: {{length}}).fill({{value}}) instead of Array.from with a constant callback
messages.preferFillSpreadMap: Use Array({{length}}).fill({{value}}) instead of spread Array with map

rules.name: prefer-array-from-map
rules.description: Prefer Array.from(iterable, mapper) over [...iterable].map(mapper)

messages.preferArrayFrom: Use Array.from({{iterable}}, {{mapper}}) instead of [...{{iterable}}].map({{mapper}}) to avoid creating an intermediate array

rules.name: prefer-includes
rules.description: Prefer .includes() over indexOf() comparisons for arrays and strings

rules.name: prefer-array-to-reversed
rules.description: Prefer Array.prototype.toReversed() over copying and reversing arrays

messages.preferToReversed: Use {{array}}.toReversed() instead of copying and reversing

rules.name: prefer-array-to-sorted
rules.description: Prefer Array.prototype.toSorted() over copying and sorting arrays

messages.preferToSorted: Use {{array}}.toSorted() instead of copying and sorting

rules.name: prefer-array-to-spliced
rules.description: Prefer Array.prototype.toSpliced() over copying and splicing arrays

messages.preferToSpliced: Use {{array}}.toSpliced() instead of copying and splicing

rules.name: prefer-exponentiation-operator
rules.description: Prefer the exponentiation operator ** over Math.pow()

messages.preferExponentiation: Use the ** operator instead of Math.pow()

rules.name: prefer-nullish-coalescing
rules.description: Prefer nullish coalescing operator (?? and ??=) over verbose null checks

messages.preferNullishCoalescing: Use nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of verbose null check
messages.preferNullishCoalescingAssignment: Use nullish coalescing assignment (??=) instead of verbose null check

rules.name: prefer-object-has-own
rules.description: Prefer Object.hasOwn() over Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() and obj.hasOwnProperty()

rules.name: prefer-spread-syntax
rules.description: Prefer spread syntax over Array.concat(), Array.from(), Object.assign({}, ...), and Function.apply()

messages.preferSpreadArray: Use spread syntax [...arr, ...other] instead of arr.concat(other)
messages.preferSpreadArrayFrom: Use spread syntax [...iterable] instead of Array.from(iterable) when no mapper function is provided
messages.preferSpreadObject: Use spread syntax {...a, ...b} instead of Object.assign({}, a, b)
messages.preferSpreadFunction: Use spread syntax fn(...args) instead of fn.apply(null/undefined, args)

rules.name: prefer-url-canparse
rules.description: Prefer URL.canParse() over try-catch blocks for URL validation

messages.preferCanParse: Use URL.canParse() instead of try-catch for URL validation

rules.name: no-indexof-equality
rules.description: Prefer optimized alternatives to indexOf() equality checks

messages.preferDirectAccess: Use direct array access `{{array}}[{{index}}] === {{item}}` instead of `indexOf() === {{index}}`

rules.name: prefer-timer-args
rules.description: Prefer passing function and arguments directly to setTimeout/setInterval

messages.preferArgs: Pass function and arguments directly to timer function to avoid allocating an extra function

rules.name: prefer-date-now
rules.description: Prefer Date.now() over Date object allocation

messages.preferDateNow: Use Date.now() instead of creating a Date object.

rules.name: prefer-regex-test
rules.description: Prefer RegExp.test() over String.match() and RegExp.exec()

rules.name: prefer-array-some
rules.description: Prefer Array.some() over Array.find() and Array.filter().length checks

messages.preferArraySome: Use Array.some() instead of Array.find() and Array.filter().length checks when checking for element existence

rules.name: prefer-static-regex
rules.description: Prefer defining regular expressions at module scope

messages.preferStatic: Move this regular expression to module scope to avoid re-compilation on every call.

rules.name: prefer-inline-equality
rules.description: Prefer inline equality checks over temporary object creation

messages.preferEquality: Avoid creating a temporary array just to call `.includes()`. Use equality checks instead.

rules.name: prefer-string-fromcharcode
rules.description: Prefer String.fromCharCode() over String.fromCodePoint() for BMP code points

messages.preferFromCharCode: String.fromCharCode is faster than String.fromCodePoint for code points below 0x10000.

rules.name: prefer-includes-over-regex-test
rules.description: Prefer string methods over simple regex tests

messages.preferIncludes: Use `String.prototype.includes()` instead of a simple regex test.
messages.preferStartsWith: Use `String.prototype.startsWith()` instead of a simple regex test.
messages.preferEndsWith: Use `String.prototype.endsWith()` instead of a simple regex test.
messages.preferEquals: Use equality instead of a simple regex test.

rules.name: no-delete-property
rules.description: Disallow delete on properties

messages.noDeleteProperty: `delete` forces V8 into a slow dictionary representation. Set the value to `undefined` if absence-vs-undefined does not matter, or use `Map` for a dynamic key-value collection.

rules.name: no-spread-in-reduce
rules.description: Disallow object/array spread inside reduce callbacks

messages.noSpreadInReduce: Avoid object/array spread inside reduce callbacks.

rules.name: prefer-static-collator
rules.description: Prefer defining Intl.Collator at module scope

messages.preferStaticCollator: Move this Intl.Collator construction to module scope to avoid re-creating it on every call.

messages.unexpected: Unexpected zero quantifier. The quantifier and its quantified element can be removed without affecting the pattern.
messages.withCapturingGroup: Unexpected zero quantifier. The quantifier and its quantified element do not affecting the pattern. Try to remove the elements but be careful because it contains at least one capturing group.

rules.name: no-octal
rules.description: disallow octal escape sequence

messages.replaceHex: Replace the octal escape sequence with a hexadecimal escape sequence.

rules.name: no-control-character
rules.description: disallow control characters

rules.name: sort-flags
rules.description: require regex flags to be sorted

messages.sortFlags: The flags '{{flags}}' should be in the order '{{sortedFlags}}'.

rules.name: require-unicode-regexp
rules.description: enforce the use of the `u` flag

rules.name: no-escape-backspace
rules.description: disallow escape backspace (`[\b]`)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected '\b' inside a character class. Use '\x08' to match the backspace character.

rules.name: prefer-plus-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `+` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-star-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `*` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-question-quantifier
rules.description: enforce using `?` quantifier

rules.name: no-useless-two-nums-quantifier
rules.description: disallow unnecessary `{n,m}` quantifier

rules.name: prefer-named-capture-group
rules.description: enforce using named capture group

messages.required: Capturing group should be converted to a named or non-capturing group.

rules.name: match-any
rules.description: enforce using `.` (with the `s` flag) instead of character classes that match any character

messages.unexpected: Unexpected any character class. Use `.` with the `s` flag instead.

rules.name: no-legacy-features
rules.description: disallow legacy `RegExp` features

messages.staticProperty: Unexpected use of the legacy 'RegExp.{{expr}}' static property; it is non-standard and not safe to rely on.

rules.name: prefer-d
rules.description: enforce using `\d` instead of `[0-9]`

rules.name: prefer-w
rules.description: enforce using `\w` instead of `[a-zA-Z0-9_]`

rules.name: letter-case
rules.description: enforce consistent case for escape sequences (default lowercase)

rules.name: no-non-standard-flag
rules.description: disallow non-standard flags on regular expressions

rules.name: no-invisible-character
rules.description: disallow invisible characters in regular expressions

rules.name: hexadecimal-escape
rules.description: disallow `\xHH` escape sequences (default `never`)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected unicode escape '{{expr}}'. Use the hexadecimal escape '{{replacement}}' instead.

rules.name: unicode-escape
rules.description: enforce using `\u{HHHH}` over `\uHHHH` (default `unicodeCodePointEscape`)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected fixed-width unicode escape '{{expr}}'. Use '{{replacement}}' instead.

rules.name: no-useless-range
rules.description: disallow character class ranges whose start equals their end

rules.name: no-empty-lookarounds-assertion
rules.description: disallow lookaround assertions with an empty body

messages.unexpected: Unexpected empty lookaround assertion; this assertion can never fail or succeed meaningfully.

rules.name: prefer-regexp-exec
rules.description: enforce `RegExp.prototype.exec` over `String.prototype.match` for non-global regexes

messages.unexpected: Use `RegExp.prototype.exec` instead of `String.prototype.match` for non-global regular expressions.

rules.name: prefer-regexp-test
rules.description: enforce `RegExp#test` instead of `String#match` / `RegExp#exec` when the result is used only as a boolean

messages.disallow: Use the `RegExp#test()` method instead, if you need a boolean.

rules.name: no-missing-g-flag
rules.description: enforce that `String.prototype.matchAll` and `replaceAll` arguments use the `g` flag

messages.unexpected: `String.prototype.{{expr}}` requires a regular expression with the 'g' flag.

rules.name: no-useless-character-class
rules.description: disallow character classes that contain only a single literal character

rules.name: no-empty-string-literal
rules.description: disallow empty string literals (`\q{}`) inside character classes

messages.unexpected: Unexpected empty string literal inside a character class.

rules.name: no-optional-assertion
rules.description: disallow optional quantifiers (`?`) immediately after a lookaround assertion

messages.unexpected: Unexpected optional lookaround assertion; an assertion does not consume input, so the `?` is meaningless.

rules.name: require-unicode-sets-regexp
rules.description: enforce the use of the `v` flag (unicode sets mode)

rules.name: confusing-quantifier
rules.description: disallow lazy quantifiers (`*?`, `??`, `{0,}?`, `{0,1}?`) whose minimum is zero

messages.unexpected: Unexpected lazy quantifier with a minimum of zero. It always prefers the empty match first.

rules.name: prefer-named-replacement
rules.description: enforce using named-group replacements (`$<name>`) when the regular expression has named captures

messages.unexpected: Use a named-group replacement (`$<name>`) instead of a numbered backreference when the regular expression has named capture groups.

rules.name: no-obscure-range
rules.description: disallow character class ranges that cross ASCII category boundaries (digits/uppercase/lowercase)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected character class range '{{expr}}' crosses character category boundaries.

rules.name: prefer-unicode-codepoint-escapes
rules.description: enforce using `\u{H+}` over surrogate-pair `\uHHHH\uHHHH` escapes

messages.unexpected: Unexpected surrogate-pair escape '{{expr}}'. Use the unicode code-point escape '{{replacement}}' instead.

rules.name: no-dupe-characters-character-class
rules.description: disallow duplicate literal characters in a character class

messages.unexpected: Duplicate character '{{expr}}' in a character class.

rules.name: prefer-range
rules.description: enforce using a range (`a-c`) instead of three or more consecutive characters

rules.name: no-useless-escape
rules.description: disallow escape sequences that have no effect on the matched character

messages.unexpected: Unnecessary escape '{{expr}}'. Use '{{replacement}}' instead — the character has no special meaning here.

rules.name: no-useless-quantifier
rules.description: disallow `{1}` and `{1,1}` quantifiers (they match exactly once and can be removed)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected quantifier '{{expr}}' that matches exactly once and can be removed.

rules.name: prefer-named-backreference
rules.description: enforce using named backreferences (`\k<name>`) when the pattern has named capture groups

messages.unexpected: Numbered backreference '{{expr}}' is used alongside a named capture group; prefer a named backreference `\k<name>`.

rules.name: no-useless-flag
rules.description: disallow regular-expression flags that have no effect on the pattern (narrow: `s` without `.`, `m` without `^`/`$`)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected useless flag '{{flag}}'; the pattern does not use the syntax this flag affects.

rules.name: no-lazy-ends
rules.description: disallow lazy quantifiers at the very end of a pattern (they prefer to match nothing)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected lazy quantifier at the end of the pattern; it will always prefer to match nothing.

rules.name: no-useless-dollar-replacements
rules.description: disallow `$0` in replacement strings (capture groups start at 1; `$0` is always literal)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected '\$0' in replacement string; `$0` is not a valid backreference (capture groups start at 1).

rules.name: prefer-escape-replacement-dollar-char
rules.description: enforce escaping a literal `$` as `$$` in replacement strings

messages.unexpected: Use '$$' to escape a literal '$' in the replacement string; a stray '$' is almost always a typo.

rules.name: use-ignore-case
rules.description: enforce the `i` flag when a character class mixes lower- and upper-case letters

messages.unexpected: Character class mixes lower- and upper-case forms of the same letter; add the 'i' flag instead.

rules.name: control-character-escape
rules.description: enforce escaping literal control characters in regular-expression patterns

messages.unexpected: Unexpected literal control character {{ char }} in the pattern; use a `\xHH` or `\uHHHH` escape sequence instead.

rules.name: grapheme-string-literal
rules.description: disallow single-character `\q{X}` string literals in v-mode character classes

messages.unexpected: Unexpected single-character string literal '{{expr}}'. Use '{{replacement}}' instead.

rules.name: no-useless-non-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow non-capturing groups that wrap a single literal character without a quantifier

messages.unexpected: Unexpected non-capturing group with a single literal body; remove the `(?:` ... `)` wrapper.

rules.name: prefer-quantifier
rules.description: apply quantifiers directly to single-character atoms instead of wrapping them in a non-capturing group

messages.unexpected: Apply the quantifier directly to the single-character body instead of wrapping it in a non-capturing group.

rules.name: no-useless-string-literal
rules.description: disallow string literals in v-mode classes when a bare character would do

messages.unexpected: Unexpected single-character string literal '{{expr}}'. Drop the `\q{...}` wrapper and use '{{replacement}}' instead.

rules.name: sort-character-class-elements
rules.description: enforce sorted character class elements when the body is all literal alphanumerics

messages.unexpected: Character class elements are not sorted; reorder them lexicographically.

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-assertion
rules.description: disallow non-capturing groups whose entire body is a single lookaround assertion

messages.unexpected: Non-capturing group whose body is exactly one lookaround assertion; drop the `(?:` ... `)` wrapper.

rules.name: no-extra-lookaround-assertions
rules.description: disallow lookaround assertions whose entire body is a single nested lookaround

messages.unexpected: Lookaround assertion whose body is exactly one nested lookaround; drop the outer assertion.

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-quantifier
rules.description: disallow trivially nested quantifiers like `(?:X+)+`

messages.unexpected: Non-capturing group with a quantified single-character body that is itself quantified; drop the wrapper and combine the quantifiers.

rules.name: prefer-character-class
rules.description: enforce using a character class instead of alternation of single literals

messages.unexpected: Alternation of single-character literals; use a character class `[abc]` instead.

rules.name: sort-alternatives
rules.description: enforce sorted single-literal alternation alternatives

messages.unexpected: Single-literal alternation is not in ascending order; sort the alternatives.

rules.name: prefer-predefined-assertion
rules.description: prefer the bare `^` or `$` anchor over a lookaround that wraps it

messages.unexpected: Lookaround whose body is exactly the `^` or `$` anchor; use the bare anchor instead.

rules.name: optimal-lookaround-quantifier
rules.description: disallow lookarounds whose body always matches because of a zero-min quantifier

messages.unexpected: Lookaround whose inner quantifier accepts the empty match; the assertion is always true.

rules.name: no-dupe-disjunctions
rules.description: disallow duplicate single-literal alternatives within a non-capturing group

messages.unexpected: Alternation contains the same single-literal alternative more than once.

rules.name: no-useless-backreference
rules.description: disallow numbered backreferences that point to a not-yet-defined capture

messages.unexpected: Backreference to a capturing group that was not yet defined when the reference appears.

rules.name: negation
rules.description: enforce use of equivalent shorthand for negated character classes containing a single predefined shorthand

messages.unexpected: Negated character class containing a single predefined shorthand can be replaced by the corresponding negated shorthand.

rules.name: no-useless-lazy
rules.description: disallow lazy modifiers on fixed-count brace quantifiers (`{n}?`, `{n,n}?`)

messages.unexpected: Lazy modifier is useless on a fixed-count quantifier; the engine always matches exactly the same number of repetitions.

rules.name: no-misleading-unicode-character
rules.description: disallow character classes that contain a ZWJ (U+200D) and match it as a separate atom

messages.unexpected: Character class contains a ZWJ (U+200D) and matches it as a separate atom; ZWJ-joined sequences cannot be matched as a single grapheme this way.

rules.name: no-standalone-backslash
rules.description: disallow standalone backslashes (`\`)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected standalone backslash (`\`). It looks like an escape sequence, but it's a single `\` character pattern.

rules.name: no-potentially-useless-backreference
rules.description: disallow backreferences to capturing groups that might not have matched (narrow: optional/star-quantified groups)

messages.potentiallyUselessBackreference: Some paths leading to the backreference do not go through the referenced capturing group or the captured text might be reset before reaching the backreference.

rules.name: strict
rules.description: disallow not strictly valid regular expressions (narrow: escape and quantifier checks)

messages.invalidControlEscape: Invalid or incomplete control escape sequence. Either use a valid control escape sequence or escape the standalone backslash.
messages.incompleteEscapeSequence: Incomplete escape sequence {{expr}}. Either use a valid escape sequence or remove the useless escaping.
messages.invalidPropertyEscape: Invalid property escape sequence {{expr}}. Either use a valid property escape sequence or remove the useless escaping.
messages.incompleteBackreference: Incomplete backreference {{expr}}. Either use a valid backreference or remove the useless escaping.
messages.octalEscape: Invalid legacy octal escape sequence {{expr}}. Use a hexadecimal escape instead.
messages.uselessEscape: Useless identity escapes with non-syntax characters are forbidden.
messages.invalidRange: Invalid character class range. A character set cannot be the minimum or maximum of a character class range.
messages.quantifiedAssertion: Assertion are not allowed to be quantified directly.

rules.name: no-useless-assertions
rules.description: disallow assertions that are known to always accept (or always reject) (narrow: word-boundary between same-class literals)

messages.unexpected: Useless word-boundary assertion: `\b`/`\B` between two characters of the same word class always rejects (`\b`) or always accepts (`\B`).

rules.name: optimal-quantifier-concatenation
rules.description: require optimal quantifiers for concatenated quantifiers (narrow: adjacent quantifiers on the same single element)

messages.unexpected: Adjacent quantifiers on the same element can be merged into a single optimal quantifier (e.g. `aa*` to `a+`).

rules.name: no-contradiction-with-assertion
rules.description: disallow elements that contradict assertions (narrow: min-zero quantifier on a same-class literal right after `\b`)

messages.unexpected: This quantifier contradicts the preceding `\b` assertion and can never be entered.

rules.name: no-useless-set-operand
rules.description: disallow unnecessary elements in expression character classes (narrow: shorthand `&&`/`--` operands that are disjoint or subsets)

messages.unexpected: This set operation has a useless operand (the operands are disjoint or one is a subset of the other).

rules.name: prefer-set-operation
rules.description: prefer character class set operations instead of lookarounds (narrow: v-mode char lookaround adjacent to a char element)

messages.unexpected: This character + lookaround can be expressed as a v-mode set operation (`[Y&&X]` or `[Y--X]`).

rules.name: simplify-set-operations
rules.description: simplify unnecessarily complex set operations (narrow: v-mode `&&` intersection with a negated nested-class operand)

messages.unexpected: This set operation can be simplified (an intersection with a negated class is a subtraction, or De Morgan applies).

rules.name: unicode-property
rules.description: enforce consistent naming of unicode properties (narrow: flag a redundant explicit `gc=` / `General_Category=` key)

messages.unnecessaryGc: Unnecessary explicit General_Category key in a Unicode property escape; drop the redundant `gc=` / `General_Category=` prefix.

rules.name: no-unused-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow unused capturing group (narrow: a capturing group in a regex literal used only as `/(...)/.test(x)`)

messages.unusedCapturingGroup: Capturing group is defined but never used.

rules.name: prefer-result-array-groups
rules.description: enforce using result array `groups` (narrow: numeric index of a match/exec result mapping to a named group)

messages.unexpected: Unexpected indexed access for a named capturing group from a regexp result array; use the `groups` property instead.

rules.name: prefer-lookaround
rules.description: prefer lookarounds over capturing groups (narrow: both-ends `(B1)MID(B2)` replace whose replacement re-emits `$1` and `$2` at the edges)

messages.preferLookarounds: These capturing groups only assert at the edges and can be replaced with lookaround assertions (`(?<=...)` and `(?=...)`).
messages.prefer: This capturing group can be replaced with a lookaround assertion.

rules.name: no-misleading-capturing-group
rules.description: disallow capturing groups that do not capture the whole pattern (narrow: `A<greedy>(A*)` where the prefix already consumes every `A`)

messages.removeQuant: This quantifier is already included by the preceding quantifier, so the capturing group misleadingly captures less text than its pattern suggests.

rules.name: no-super-linear-backtracking
rules.description: disallow exponential and polynomial backtracking (narrow: nested unbounded quantifiers `(X+)+` and similar)

messages.self: This quantifier can reach itself through the surrounding loop, causing exponential backtracking.
messages.trade: This quantifier exchanges characters with another quantifier, causing polynomial backtracking.

rules.name: no-super-linear-move
rules.description: disallow quantifiers that cause quadratic moves (narrow: a leading unbounded quantifier with a required rejecting suffix)

messages.unexpected: This leading unbounded quantifier with a rejecting suffix causes quadratic scanning time because the quantifier is retried from every start position.

plugins.description: Rust-backed oxlint plugin port of eslint-plugin-security.

rules.name: detect-bidi-characters
rules.description: Detects trojan source attacks that employ unicode bidi attacks to inject malicious code.

messages.code: Detected potential trojan source attack with unicode bidi introduced in this code: '{{text}}'.
messages.comment: Detected potential trojan source attack with unicode bidi introduced in this comment: '{{text}}'.

rules.name: detect-buffer-noassert
rules.description: Detects calls to "buffer" with "noAssert" flag set.

messages.found: Found Buffer.{{method}} with noAssert flag set true

rules.name: detect-child-process
rules.description: Detects instances of "child_process" & non-literal "exec()" calls.

messages.execNonLiteral: Found child_process.exec() with non Literal first argument

rules.name: detect-disable-mustache-escape
rules.description: Detects "object.escapeMarkup = false", which can be used with some template engines to disable escaping of HTML entities.

rules.name: detect-eval-with-expression
rules.description: Detects "eval(variable)" which can allow an attacker to run arbitrary code inside your process.

rules.name: detect-new-buffer
rules.description: Detects instances of new Buffer(argument) where argument is any non-literal value.

rules.name: detect-no-csrf-before-method-override
rules.description: Detects Express "csrf" middleware setup before "method-override" middleware.

rules.name: detect-non-literal-fs-filename
rules.description: Detects variable in filename argument of "fs" calls, which might allow an attacker to access anything on your system.

messages.nonLiteral: Found {{fnName}} from package "{{packageName}}" with non literal argument at index {{indices}}

rules.name: detect-non-literal-regexp
rules.description: Detects "RegExp(variable)", which might allow an attacker to DOS your server with a long-running regular expression.

messages.nonLiteral: Found non-literal argument to RegExp Constructor

rules.name: detect-non-literal-require
rules.description: Detects "require(variable)", which might allow an attacker to load and run arbitrary code, or access arbitrary files on disk.

rules.name: detect-object-injection
rules.description: Detects "variable[key]" as a left- or right-hand assignment operand.

messages.variable: Variable Assigned to Object Injection Sink

rules.name: detect-possible-timing-attacks
rules.description: Detects insecure comparisons (`==`, `!=`, `!==` and `===`), which check input sequentially.

rules.name: detect-pseudoRandomBytes
rules.description: Detects if "pseudoRandomBytes()" is in use, which might not give you the randomness you need and expect.

messages.found: Found crypto.pseudoRandomBytes which does not produce cryptographically strong numbers

rules.name: detect-unsafe-regex
rules.description: Detects potentially unsafe regular expressions, which may take a very long time to run, blocking the event loop.

plugins.description: Rust-backed Oxlint plugin port of eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort.

rules.name: exports
rules.description: automatically sort exports

messages.sort: Run autofix to sort these exports!

rules.name: imports
rules.description: automatically sort imports

messages.sort: Run autofix to sort these imports!

plugins.description: Rust-backed Oxlint plugin port of eslint-plugin-sonarjs (clean-room; upstream is LGPL-3.0 and is not redistributed).

rules.name: no-nested-template-literals
rules.description: Disallow nested template literals

messages.nestedTemplateLiteral: Do not nest template literals. Extract the inner template literal into a separate variable.

rules.name: no-nested-switch
rules.description: Disallow nested switch statements

messages.nestedSwitch: Do not nest switch statements. Extract the nested switch into a separate function.

rules.name: no-nested-conditional
rules.description: Disallow nested conditional (ternary) expressions

messages.nestedConditional: Do not nest ternary/conditional expressions; extract the nested conditional into an independent statement.

rules.name: no-collapsible-if
rules.description: Disallow collapsible if statements that should be merged

messages.collapsibleIf: Merge this 'if' statement with the nested one to reduce nesting.

rules.name: no-redundant-boolean
rules.description: Disallow redundant boolean literals in expressions

rules.name: comma-or-logical-or-case
rules.description: Disallow '||' or ',' expressions as switch case labels

messages.commaOrLogicalOrInCase: This 'case' label uses '||' or ',', which does not compare against multiple values as it appears to.

rules.name: no-duplicate-in-composite
rules.description: Disallow duplicate type members in TypeScript union or intersection types

messages.duplicateType: Remove this duplicated type or replace with another one.

rules.name: non-existent-operator
rules.description: Disallow the suspicious '=-', '=+', or '=!' operator typos adjacent to a plain assignment

messages.nonExistentOperator: Was this '=-', '=+', or '=!' meant to be a compound assignment or comparison? Add a space to clarify, or fix the operator.

rules.name: no-identical-conditions
rules.description: Disallow duplicate conditions in the same if/else-if chain (dead branch)

messages.identicalConditions: This branch's condition duplicates an earlier one in the same if/else-if chain, so it can never be reached.

rules.name: no-all-duplicated-branches
rules.description: Disallow conditional structures where every branch has the same implementation

messages.allDuplicatedBranches: Remove this conditional structure or edit its code blocks so that they are not all the same.

rules.name: no-identical-expressions
rules.description: Disallow identical sub-expressions on both sides of binary or logical operators where the result is constant or redundant

messages.identicalExpressions: Identical sub-expressions on both sides of this operator make the result constant or redundant.

rules.name: arguments-usage
rules.description: Disallow use of the 'arguments' object; use rest parameters instead

messages.argumentsUsage: Use the rest parameter syntax (...args) instead of the 'arguments' object.

rules.name: no-labels
rules.description: Disallow labeled statements; use structured control flow instead

messages.noLabels: Remove this label and refactor the code to use structured control flow instead.

rules.name: label-position
rules.description: Disallow labels on statements other than loops and switch statements

messages.removeLabel: Remove this label or move it directly onto a loop or switch statement.

rules.name: no-delete-var
rules.description: Disallow 'delete' applied to a plain variable; use it only on object properties

messages.noDeleteVar: Do not use 'delete' on a variable; it has no effect. Use 'delete' only to remove object properties.

rules.name: constructor-for-side-effects
rules.description: Disallow using new solely for side effects without capturing or using the constructed object

messages.constructorForSideEffects: Either use this object, assign it to a variable, or move the side effects into a named function instead of a constructor.

rules.name: no-empty-character-class
rules.description: Disallow empty character classes in regular expression literals, which can never match

messages.emptyCharacterClass: This empty character class [] can never match, so this regular expression will never match anything.

rules.name: generator-without-yield
rules.description: Disallow generator functions that contain no 'yield' expression and therefore behave like plain functions

messages.generatorWithoutYield: This generator contains no 'yield'; either add a 'yield' or convert it to a regular function.

rules.name: no-exclusive-tests
rules.description: Disallow .only on test-runner functions (describe, it, test, etc.) that would disable all other tests

messages.noExclusiveTests: Remove '.only' so the whole test suite runs, not just this test.

rules.name: no-built-in-override
rules.description: Disallow overriding or shadowing standard ECMAScript built-in global objects and functions

messages.noBuiltInOverride: Do not override or shadow a built-in object or function.

rules.name: class-prototype
rules.description: Disallow assigning methods or properties to a constructor prototype; use class syntax instead

messages.classPrototype: Define this on a class using method syntax instead of assigning to the prototype.

rules.name: max-switch-cases
rules.description: Disallow switch statements with more than the configured number of case/default clauses (the "maximum" option; default 30)

messages.maxSwitchCases: This switch has too many cases; consider a lookup table or polymorphism.

rules.name: max-union-size
rules.description: Disallow union types with more than the configured number of members (the "threshold" option; default 3; each TSUnionType node is counted per-node)

messages.maxUnionSize: This union type has too many members; consider refactoring into a named type or interface.

rules.name: elseif-without-else
rules.description: Require a final 'else' clause when an 'if … else if' chain is present, to explicitly handle the remaining case

messages.elseifWithoutElse: Add a final 'else' clause to this 'if … else if' chain to handle the remaining cases explicitly.

rules.name: no-case-label-in-switch
rules.description: Disallow labeled statements appearing directly in a switch case's consequent list, where they are likely mistaken 'case' clauses

messages.caseLabelInSwitch: Remove this misleading label; it looks like a 'case' clause but is a labeled statement.

rules.name: for-in
rules.description: Require a 'for...in' loop body to be a single 'if' statement that filters inherited properties (structural check only — the 'if' condition is not inspected)

messages.forIn: Wrap this 'for...in' loop body in an 'if' statement to filter out inherited properties.

rules.name: prefer-while
rules.description: Disallow 'for' loops with no initializer and no update clause; use a 'while' loop instead

messages.preferWhile: Replace this 'for' loop with a 'while' loop; it has no initializer or update clause.

rules.name: no-small-switch
rules.description: Disallow switch statements with fewer than two real 'case' clauses; use an 'if' statement instead (default clause not counted)

messages.smallSwitch: This switch has too few cases; use an 'if' statement instead.

rules.name: prefer-default-last
rules.description: Require the 'default' clause of a switch statement to appear as the last clause for readability

messages.defaultLast: Move this 'default' clause to the end of the switch statement.

rules.name: no-inverted-boolean-check
rules.description: Disallow negating a comparison expression; use the opposite comparison operator instead

messages.invertedBooleanCheck: Use the opposite comparison operator instead of negating this comparison.

rules.name: no-useless-catch
rules.description: Disallow 'catch' clauses that only rethrow the caught exception; remove them and let the error propagate naturally

messages.uselessCatch: Remove this useless 'catch' clause; it only rethrows the caught exception.

rules.name: no-redundant-optional
rules.description: Disallow optional property signatures whose type annotation already includes 'undefined'; the '?' marker already permits undefined

messages.redundantOptional: Remove this redundant 'undefined' type; the '?' optional marker already allows it.

rules.name: prefer-immediate-return
rules.description: Disallow declaring a local variable solely to immediately return or throw it; return or throw the initializer expression directly

messages.preferImmediateReturn: Return or throw this expression directly instead of assigning it to a temporary variable first.

rules.name: no-redundant-jump
rules.description: Disallow jump statements (continue without label, return without value) that do not change the control flow because execution would proceed the same way anyway

messages.redundantJump: Remove this redundant jump; it does not change the control flow.

rules.name: no-primitive-wrappers
rules.description: Disallow using 'new' with the primitive wrapper constructors Number, String, or Boolean, which create wrapper objects instead of primitive values

messages.primitiveWrapper: Use the primitive type instead of the 'new Number/String/Boolean' wrapper object.

rules.name: no-skipped-tests
rules.description: Disallow committed skipped tests (.skip member or x-prefixed Jasmine calls); re-enable or remove them instead

messages.skippedTest: Re-enable or remove this skipped test.

rules.name: prefer-single-boolean-return
rules.description: Disallow if/else structures where both branches return a boolean literal; return the condition directly instead

messages.preferSingleBooleanReturn: Replace this if/else returning booleans with a single 'return' of the condition.

rules.name: no-unthrown-error
rules.description: Disallow creating an Error (or Error subtype) with 'new' as a bare statement without throwing it; the value is discarded and this is almost always a bug

messages.unthrownError: Throw this error or remove this useless statement.

rules.name: no-tab
rules.description: Disallow tab characters in source files; tabs render inconsistently across editors and tools, so spaces should be used instead

messages.noTab: Replace this tab character with spaces.

rules.name: fixme-tag
rules.description: Disallow FIXME-tagged comments; a FIXME marks code that is known-broken and must be addressed before shipping

rules.name: todo-tag
rules.description: Disallow TODO-tagged comments; a TODO marks incomplete work that should be tracked and completed

messages.todoTag: Complete the task tracked by this TODO-tagged comment.

rules.name: no-sonar-comments
rules.description: Disallow NOSONAR comments; they suppress analysis and can hide real issues that should be fixed

messages.noSonarComments: Remove this NOSONAR comment and fix the underlying issue.

rules.name: array-constructor
rules.description: Disallow the Array constructor in favor of array literals, except for the single-argument length form

messages.arrayConstructor: Use an array literal instead of the Array constructor.

rules.name: no-function-declaration-in-block
rules.description: Disallow function declarations nested directly inside a block; use a function expression or move it to the top level

messages.noFunctionDeclarationInBlock: Move this function declaration out of the block, or use a function expression instead.

rules.name: no-inconsistent-returns
rules.description: Disallow mixing value returns and bare returns in the same function; return a value on all paths or none

messages.inconsistentReturns: Refactor this function to use "return" consistently, either always with a value or always without.

rules.name: no-same-line-conditional
rules.description: Disallow an "if" statement placed on the same line as the closing brace of a preceding sibling "if"

messages.sameLineConditional: Move this "if" to a new line or add the missing "else" to clarify the intent.

rules.name: no-nested-assignment
rules.description: Disallow assignments inside sub-expressions such as loop and branch conditions or chained assignments

messages.nestedAssignment: Extract this assignment out of the expression into its own statement.

rules.name: no-nested-incdec
rules.description: Disallow increment and decrement operators used as a function or constructor call argument

messages.nestedIncDec: Extract this increment or decrement operator into a separate statement.

rules.name: no-useless-increment
rules.description: Disallow assigning a postfix increment or decrement of a variable back to that same variable

messages.uselessIncrement: Remove this useless increment or decrement; the updated value is immediately discarded.

rules.name: class-name
rules.description: Require class names to start with an uppercase letter, following the PascalCase convention

messages.className: Rename this class to start with an uppercase letter (PascalCase).

rules.name: function-name
rules.description: Require function and method names to match the configured regular expression

messages.renameFunction: Rename this function "{{value}}" to match the regular expression "{{format}}".

rules.name: max-lines
rules.description: Disallow files with more code lines than the configured maximum (the "maximum" option; default 1000); blank lines and comment-only lines are not counted

messages.maxLines: This file has more lines than the maximum allowed; split it into smaller files.

rules.name: nested-control-flow
rules.description: Disallow control flow statements (if/for/for-in/for-of/while/do-while/switch/try) nested beyond the configured maximumNestingLevel (default 3); else-if chains do not add a level

messages.nestedControlFlow: Refactor this code to reduce the nesting of control flow statements.

rules.name: max-lines-per-function
rules.description: Disallow functions with more code lines than the configured maximum (the "maximum" option; default 200); IIFEs and JSX-containing functions are excluded

messages.maxLinesPerFunction: This function has more lines than the maximum allowed; split it into smaller functions.

rules.name: no-duplicate-string
rules.description: Disallow string literals of 10+ characters containing a non-word character from appearing at least threshold (default 3) times in a file; import/export sources and JSX attribute values are excluded

messages.duplicateString: Define this repeated string literal as a constant to avoid duplication.

rules.name: no-empty-group
rules.description: Disallow empty capturing or non-capturing groups in regular expression literals

messages.emptyGroup: Remove this empty group or add content to it.

rules.name: no-empty-alternatives
rules.description: Disallow empty alternatives in a regular expression alternation (a stray, leading, or trailing "|")

messages.emptyAlternative: Remove this empty alternative or replace the alternation with an optional.

rules.name: no-regex-spaces
rules.description: Disallow multiple consecutive spaces in a regular expression; use an explicit quantifier instead

messages.multipleSpaces: Use a quantifier (e.g. " {3}") instead of multiple consecutive spaces.

rules.name: no-control-regex
rules.description: Disallow control characters written as \x, \u, or \c escapes in regular expressions

messages.controlCharacter: Remove this control character from the regular expression or write it as a conventional escape.

rules.name: single-char-in-character-classes
rules.description: Disallow a regular-expression character class that contains only a single literal character

messages.singleCharInCharacterClass: Replace this single-character class with the character itself.

rules.name: duplicates-in-character-class
rules.description: Disallow the same literal character appearing more than once in a regular-expression character class

messages.duplicateCharacter: Remove this duplicated character from the character class.

rules.name: anchor-precedence
rules.description: Disallow regex alternations where ^ or $ anchors only one branch due to operator precedence

messages.anchorPrecedence: Group the alternatives or add anchors to each branch to make operator precedence explicit.

rules.name: cyclomatic-complexity
rules.description: Disallow functions whose cyclomatic complexity exceeds the configured threshold (the "threshold" option; default 10); each if/for/while/do-while/case/catch/ternary/logical-operator adds +1

messages.cyclomaticComplexity: Refactor this function to reduce its cyclomatic complexity.

rules.name: no-collection-size-mischeck
rules.description: Disallow comparisons of collection .length or .size against 0 with < or >= that are always false or always true

messages.collectionSizeMischeck: This size/length comparison is always true or always false; fix the comparison.

rules.name: index-of-compare-to-positive-number
rules.description: Disallow comparing the result of indexOf or lastIndexOf against a positive number, which silently excludes the element at index 0

messages.indexOfPositive: This "indexOf" check ignores index 0; compare against -1 or use ">= 0" instead.

rules.name: no-nested-functions
rules.description: Disallow functions nested more deeply than the configured threshold (the "threshold" option; default 4); applies to function declarations, function expressions, and arrow functions

messages.noNestedFunctions: This function is nested too deeply. Refactor to reduce nesting depth.

rules.name: too-many-break-or-continue-in-loop
rules.description: Disallow more than one break or continue statement targeting the same loop; having two or more jumps makes control flow hard to follow

messages.tooManyBreakContinue: Reduce the total number of break and continue statements in this loop to at most one.

rules.name: code-eval
rules.description: Disallow dynamic code execution via eval() or the Function constructor, which can introduce security vulnerabilities

messages.codeEval: Review this use of dynamic code execution; it can introduce security vulnerabilities.

rules.name: void-use
rules.description: Disallow the 'void' operator; write 'undefined' directly or restructure the code to avoid discarding values

messages.voidUse: Remove this use of the "void" operator.

rules.name: prefer-promise-shorthand
rules.description: Disallow new Promise(executor) when the executor immediately calls resolve or reject with at most one argument; use Promise.resolve or Promise.reject instead

messages.preferShorthand: Replace this trivial promise with Promise.resolve or Promise.reject.

rules.name: pseudo-random
rules.description: Disallow Math.random() in security-sensitive contexts; use a cryptographically secure random number generator instead

messages.pseudoRandom: Use a cryptographically secure random number generator instead of Math.random().

rules.name: hashing
rules.description: Disallow obsolete hashing algorithms such as MD5 and SHA-1 in crypto hashing APIs

messages.weakHash: Use a stronger hashing algorithm for security-sensitive data.

rules.name: no-clear-text-protocols
rules.description: Disallow clear-text URL protocols such as http, ftp, telnet, ws, and ldap in string literals

messages.clearTextProtocol: Use an encrypted protocol instead of this clear-text URL.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-ip
rules.description: Disallow hardcoded IP addresses in string literals; make them configurable instead

messages.hardcodedIp: Make this IP address configurable rather than hardcoding it in source code.

rules.name: no-global-this
rules.description: Disallow references to the global "this" object outside of any function or class scope that rebinds "this"; prefer accessing global properties directly

messages.noGlobalThis: Remove this use of the global "this" object.

rules.name: single-character-alternation
rules.description: Disallow regex alternations where every alternative is a single character; use a character class instead

messages.singleCharAlternation: Replace this alternation of single characters with a character class.

rules.name: empty-string-repetition
rules.description: Disallow repetition quantifiers applied to a sub-pattern that can match the empty string

messages.emptyStringRepetition: Rework this part of the regex to not repeat an expression that can match the empty string.

rules.name: no-misleading-array-reverse
rules.description: Disallow using the return value of the in-place array-mutating methods "reverse" and "sort" as if they returned a new array

messages.misleadingReverse: Move this array "reverse" operation to a separate statement or operate on a copy; it mutates the original array in place.

rules.name: no-alphabetical-sort
rules.description: Require a compare function when calling "sort" or "toSorted" on an array

messages.provideCompareFunction: Provide a compare function to avoid sorting elements alphabetically.

rules.name: no-for-in-iterable
rules.description: Disallow "for...in" loops over arrays; use a "for...of" loop to iterate values instead

messages.noForInIterable: Use a "for...of" loop instead of a "for...in" loop to iterate over this array.

rules.name: no-associative-arrays
rules.description: Disallow assigning non-numeric keys to arrays; use an object or a Map instead

messages.noAssociativeArray: Use an object or a Map instead of this array with non-numeric keys.

rules.name: bitwise-operators
rules.description: Disallow a bitwise "&" or "|" whose operand is a boolean-valued expression (comparison, logical, "!", or boolean literal), which is likely a typo for "&&" or "||"

messages.bitwiseOperator: Review this use of a bitwise operator; "&&" or "||" may have been intended.

rules.name: no-same-argument-assert
rules.description: Disallow a Chai "assert.<method>(...)" call whose first two arguments are the same source expression, since it compares a value to itself and is trivially true

messages.sameArgumentAssert: Replace this assertion; the actual and expected arguments are the same expression.

rules.name: inverted-assertion-arguments
rules.description: Disallow a Chai "assert.<method>(...)" call whose first argument is a literal constant and second is not, since the actual/expected arguments were likely inverted

messages.invertedArguments: Swap these assertion arguments so the actual value comes first and the expected value second.

rules.name: for-loop-increment-sign
rules.description: Disallow a "for" loop whose update clause moves the counter away from the relational termination condition (e.g. "i < n" with "i--"), which can prevent the loop from terminating

messages.wrongDirection: This loop update moves the counter away from the termination condition, so the loop may not stop as intended.

rules.name: no-equals-in-for-termination
rules.description: Disallow an equality operator ("==", "!=", "===", "!==") in a "for" loop termination condition when the counter is advanced by a non-unit step (e.g. "i != 10" with "i += 2"), which can skip the bound and loop forever

messages.noEqualsInForTermination: Replace this equality operator in the loop termination condition with a relational operator.

rules.name: reduce-initial-value
rules.description: Require an initial value (second argument) when calling "reduce" or "reduceRight" on an array, to avoid a TypeError on empty arrays and a skipped first element

messages.provideInitialValue: Provide an initial value to this "reduce" call.

rules.name: no-parameter-reassignment
rules.description: Disallow reassigning a function parameter, caught exception, or for-in/for-of loop variable, which discards the value supplied at runtime; introduce a new local variable instead

messages.noParameterReassignment: Introduce a new variable instead of reassigning this parameter.

rules.name: array-callback-without-return
rules.description: Require a return statement in callbacks of array methods that build a result

messages.addReturn: Add a "return" statement to this callback.

rules.name: declarations-in-global-scope
rules.description: Disallow function and var declarations that create global or module-scope bindings

messages.defineLocally: Move this declaration into a local scope, or attach it explicitly to the global object.

rules.name: no-wildcard-import
rules.description: Disallow wildcard (namespace) imports such as 'import * as ns from "mod"'; import only the specific members you need to keep code readable and tree-shakeable

messages.noWildcardImport: Import only the specific members you need instead of the whole namespace.

rules.name: updated-loop-counter
rules.description: Disallow updating a classic "for" loop counter inside the loop body (reassignment, compound assignment, or increment/decrement); advance the counter only in the update clause

messages.noCounterUpdate: Do not update the loop counter inside the loop body; use the for-statement's update clause.

rules.name: misplaced-loop-counter
rules.description: Disallow a classic "for" loop whose update clause modifies only variable(s) absent from the loop condition (e.g. "i < 10" with "j++"), since the tested counter is never advanced by the header

messages.misplacedCounter: This loop's update clause does not modify any variable checked in its condition.

rules.name: no-array-delete
rules.description: Disallow the "delete" operator on array elements (e.g. "delete arr[0]"), which leaves a hole without updating the array length; use Array.prototype.splice() instead

messages.noArrayDelete: Use Array.prototype.splice() to remove this element instead of the delete operator.

rules.name: no-literal-call
rules.description: Disallow calling a literal as a function or using it as a tagged-template tag (e.g. "true()" or "true`x`"); a literal is never callable and always throws a TypeError at runtime

messages.noLiteralCall: This literal cannot be called as a function and throws a TypeError at runtime.

rules.name: shorthand-property-grouping
rules.description: Require shorthand object properties to be grouped as one contiguous block at the beginning or end of the object literal, rather than interleaved with regular "key: value" properties

messages.groupShorthand: Group all shorthand properties together at the beginning or end of this object declaration.

rules.name: process-argv
rules.description: Flag reads of process.argv, since using command-line arguments is security-sensitive and should be reviewed

messages.processArgv: Make sure that reading command-line arguments is safe here.

rules.name: standard-input
rules.description: Flag reads of process.stdin, since reading from the standard input is security-sensitive and should be reviewed

messages.standardInput: Make sure that reading from the standard input is safe here.

rules.name: no-code-after-done
rules.description: Flag statements that run after a "done()" callback call in a Mocha test or hook, since the test is already finished and the trailing code executes unexpectedly

messages.noCodeAfterDone: Refactor this test; the code after the "done()" call will run unexpectedly.

rules.name: function-inside-loop
rules.description: Flag functions (declarations, expressions, or arrow functions) defined inside a loop, since such closures are error-prone and inefficient; immediately invoked function expressions are exempt

messages.noFunctionInLoop: Refactor this code; do not define functions inside loops.

rules.name: no-useless-intersection
rules.description: Disallow a TypeScript intersection type that contains an "any", "never", or "unknown" keyword member, which collapses or is absorbed by the intersection and makes it pointless (syntactic keyword cases only; type-aware subtype/supertype redundancy is out of scope)

messages.uselessIntersection: Remove this "any", "never", or "unknown" member; it makes the whole intersection type pointless.

rules.name: use-type-alias
rules.description: Require extracting a union or intersection type into a named type alias when the same composite type (compared by exact source text, order-sensitive) appears at least 3 times in a file; the first occurrence of each repeated type is reported

messages.useTypeAlias: Replace this repeated union/intersection type with a named type alias.

rules.name: public-static-readonly
rules.description: Require a publicly accessible static class field (explicit "public" or no accessibility modifier) to be declared "readonly"; static fields that are private, protected, readonly, declared with a "#private" key, or non-static are not flagged (modifier-based; applies to both JS and TS source)

rules.name: call-argument-line
rules.description: Require a function call's opening parenthesis (and therefore its argument list) to begin on the same line as the end of the callee; writing the call's parentheses on the line below the function name is reported, while wrapping the arguments across lines with the open parenthesis still on the callee's line is allowed

messages.sameLineAsCallee: Make the arguments of this call start on the same line as the function name.

rules.name: prefer-object-literal
rules.description: Require an object to be created and initialized with a single object literal rather than declared as an empty object and then populated with property assignments; flags an empty object-literal declaration whose immediately following sibling statement assigns to a property of that same variable

messages.preferObjectLiteral: Declare this object with its properties in a single object literal.

rules.name: no-undefined-argument
rules.description: Disallow passing the literal 'undefined' as the last argument to a function or constructor call; omitting it is equivalent and avoids redundant noise

rules.name: no-identical-functions
rules.description: Disallow two functions in the same file whose parameter list and body are byte-for-byte identical; such duplication is almost always a copy-paste mistake or a missed abstraction (functions spanning fewer than 3 lines are exempt)

messages.identicalFunctions: Update this function so that its implementation is not identical to the one on line {{value}}.

rules.name: no-in-misuse
rules.description: Disallow using the 'in' operator to test whether a value is an element of an array; use 'Array.prototype.includes' or 'Array.prototype.indexOf' instead, because 'in' checks property keys, not values

messages.inMisuse: Use 'indexOf' or 'includes' to check for the presence of a value in this array; the 'in' operator only checks property keys.

rules.name: no-require-or-define
rules.description: Disallow CommonJS 'require()' calls and AMD 'define()' calls; use ES module 'import' statements instead

messages.noRequireOrDefine: Use a standard 'import' statement instead of 'require' or 'define'.

rules.name: no-invalid-regexp
rules.description: Disallow a syntactically invalid regular expression pattern or flag string passed as a string literal to the RegExp constructor; applies to both new RegExp(...) and RegExp(...) call forms

messages.invalidRegExp: This regular expression is invalid; fix the pattern (or flags) passed to RegExp.

rules.name: no-invariant-returns
rules.description: Disallow functions that always return the same value regardless of their logic; the return value should depend on the function's input or state

messages.invariantReturn: This function always returns the same value; the return value does not depend on the function's logic.

rules.name: no-extra-arguments
rules.description: Disallow calling a function with more arguments than the function declares parameters (conservative: only const/let/var-assigned function expressions and arrow functions are checked)

messages.extraArguments: This function is called with more arguments than it declares ({{value}}).

rules.name: link-with-target-blank
rules.description: Require <a> and <area> JSX elements with target="_blank" to also carry a rel attribute containing "noopener" or "noreferrer", to prevent the opened page from accessing window.opener (reverse-tabnabbing)

messages.targetBlankNoOpener: Add 'rel="noopener"' (or 'noreferrer') to this link with target="_blank" to prevent the opened page from accessing 'window.opener'.

rules.name: no-weak-cipher
rules.description: Disallow weak cipher algorithms such as DES, RC2, RC4, Blowfish, and IDEA in Node-style crypto cipher APIs

messages.weakCipher: Use a modern cipher instead of this weak cipher algorithm.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-passwords
rules.description: Disallow hardcoded password string literals assigned to a password-named identifier; provide credentials via configuration or environment variables instead

messages.hardcodedPassword: Remove this hardcoded password; provide credentials via configuration or environment instead.

rules.name: no-ignored-exceptions
rules.description: Disallow empty catch blocks that silently swallow exceptions; at minimum log or rethrow the exception, or add a comment explaining why it is safe to ignore

messages.ignoredException: Handle this exception or explain in a comment why it can be safely ignored.

rules.name: no-unused-function-argument
rules.description: Disallow trailing function parameters that are never referenced; parameters that appear before a used parameter are exempt (they cannot be removed without changing call sites)

messages.unusedFunctionArgument: Remove this unused trailing function parameter or rename it with a leading underscore to mark it as intentional.

rules.name: object-alt-content
rules.description: Require <object> JSX elements to provide an accessible text alternative via child content, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title; elements explicitly hidden with aria-hidden="true" are exempt

messages.objectAltContent: Add an accessible text alternative to this <object> element (child content, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title).

rules.name: no-use-of-empty-return-value
rules.description: Disallow using the return value of a function that does not explicitly return a value; such a function always returns undefined, so consuming its result is almost always a bug

messages.useOfEmptyReturnValue: Remove this use of the return value of a function that does not return anything.

rules.name: no-duplicated-branches
rules.description: Disallow any two branches in an if/else-if/else chain, or any two case/default clauses in a switch statement, from having byte-identical implementations; merge or differentiate them

messages.duplicatedBranch: This branch's implementation is duplicated on another branch; merge or differentiate them.

rules.name: block-scoped-var
rules.description: Disallow 'var' declarations inside a block (if/for/while/do/switch/bare-block) when the binding is used outside that block; use block-scoped let/const or declare the variable in the enclosing scope

messages.blockScopedVar: Declare this variable in the enclosing scope, or use 'let'/'const' — it is a 'var' used outside the block where it is declared.

rules.name: no-variable-usage-before-declaration
rules.description: Disallow referencing a variable before its var/let/const declaration appears in the source text; function declarations are excluded because their hoisting is intentional

messages.usedBeforeDeclaration: This variable is used before its declaration; declare it before use.

rules.name: arguments-order
rules.description: Disallow calling a function with arguments that match the parameter names but in a transposed (swapped) order; detects only calls where every argument is a plain identifier whose name is one of the declared parameter names, reordered

messages.argumentsOrder: These arguments match the parameter names but are passed in a different order; check for swapped arguments.

rules.name: updated-const-var
rules.description: Disallow assigning to a const binding, including update expressions, destructuring assignments, and for-in/for-of assignment targets

messages.updateConst: Correct this attempt to modify "{{value}}" or use "let" in its declaration.

rules.name: unicode-aware-regex
rules.description: Disallow Unicode property escapes (\p{...} or \P{...}) in regular expressions that lack the 'u' or 'v' flag, since without those flags the engine treats \p as a literal 'p'

messages.unicodeAwareRegex: Add the 'u' flag to this regular expression so its Unicode property escape (\p{...}) works correctly.

rules.name: no-undefined-assignment
rules.description: Disallow explicitly assigning the bare identifier 'undefined' in an assignment expression; use 'null' to clear a value or leave the variable uninitialized instead

messages.noUndefinedAssignment: Do not explicitly assign 'undefined'; use 'null' or leave the variable uninitialized.

rules.name: no-empty-after-reluctant
rules.description: Disallow a reluctant (lazy) quantifier that can match the empty string when every following term in the same alternative is also optional or absent, making the quantifier always match empty

messages.emptyAfterReluctant: This reluctant quantifier will always match the empty string because everything that follows it is optional or there is nothing after it; review the pattern.

rules.name: no-ignored-return
rules.description: Disallow discarding the return value of a pure built-in method call when the receiver is a literal of a statically-known type (string, number, or array literal); restricted to literal receivers only to avoid false positives in the absence of type information

messages.ignoredReturn: The return value of this pure method call is discarded; use the result or remove the call.

rules.name: file-name-differ-from-class
rules.description: Require that a file exporting exactly one named class be named after that class; the comparison strips hyphens and underscores and ignores case so that "MyClass", "my-class", and "my_class" all match each other

messages.fileNameDifferFromClass: Rename this file to match the name of the class it exports, or rename the class to match the file.

rules.name: no-unenclosed-multiline-block
rules.description: Disallow a sibling statement that is indented as if it belongs to a preceding unbraced control-structure body (if/for/while/else without braces), since it always executes unconditionally despite appearing guarded

messages.unenclosedMultilineBlock: This line is indented as if it belongs to the preceding unbraced block, but it does not; add braces or fix the indentation.

rules.name: inconsistent-function-call
rules.description: Disallow calling a function both as a plain call and as a constructor (with new) within the same file; pick one calling convention and apply it consistently

messages.inconsistentFunctionCall: This function is invoked both as a constructor (with 'new') and as a plain function; use it consistently.

rules.name: new-operator-misuse
rules.description: Disallow using 'new' with an arrow function; arrow functions cannot be constructors and always throw a TypeError

messages.newOperatorMisuse: Do not use 'new' with an arrow function; arrow functions are not constructors and this throws a TypeError.

rules.name: no-empty-test-file
rules.description: Require test files (whose name contains .test. or .spec.) to contain at least one it() or test() call; a test file with no test cases is always a bug

messages.emptyTestFile: This test file does not contain any test cases ('it'/'test').

rules.name: deprecation
rules.description: Disallow using a same-file function or class whose declaration is immediately preceded by a block comment containing @deprecated; covers only local declarations to avoid false positives in the absence of cross-module type information

messages.deprecatedUse: Do not use code that is marked as deprecated; replace it with the recommended alternative.

rules.name: cognitive-complexity
rules.description: Disallow functions whose Cognitive Complexity exceeds the configured threshold (the "threshold" option; default 15); nesting depth is factored into the increment for each structural element

messages.cognitiveComplexity: Refactor this function to reduce its Cognitive Complexity.

rules.name: expression-complexity
rules.description: Disallow expressions with more than the configured number of logical (&&, ||, ??) and conditional (?:) operators (the "threshold" option; default 3); operators inside nested function or arrow-function bodies are counted independently

messages.expressionComplexity: Refactor this expression to reduce the number of logical and conditional operators.

rules.name: prefer-regexp-exec
rules.description: Prefer RegExp.prototype.exec() over String.prototype.match() when matching a non-global regular-expression literal

messages.preferRegExpExec: Use RegExp.prototype.exec() instead of String.prototype.match() for a non-global regular expression.

rules.name: no-fallthrough
rules.description: Require non-empty switch cases to end with an unconditional jump, unless an intentional fallthrough comment is present

messages.noFallthrough: End this switch case with an unconditional jump, or add an intentional fallthrough comment.

rules.name: no-commented-code
rules.description: Disallow sections of code that have been commented out; commented-out code makes files harder to read and should be removed

rules.name: no-incomplete-assertions
rules.description: Disallow a Chai BDD "expect(value)" chain used as a statement that never reaches a terminal assertion property or method call, so the test always passes silently

messages.incompleteAssertion: Complete this assertion: add a terminal assertion property or method call after "expect(...)".

rules.name: destructuring-assignment-syntax
rules.description: Require destructuring when two or more consecutive single-declarator const/let statements each extract a property from the same plain identifier and the binding name matches the property name (e.g. const a = obj.a; const b = obj.b; → const { a, b } = obj;)

messages.useDestructuring: Use destructuring to merge these consecutive property extractions into a single declaration.

rules.name: no-element-overwrite
rules.description: Disallow writing to the same collection element (array index or object property) twice in consecutive statements with no read in between; the first write is dead and is almost always a bug (wrong index or key)

messages.elementOverwrite: This collection element is overwritten before it is read; the earlier assignment is dead.

rules.name: no-redundant-assignments
rules.description: Disallow assignments whose value is never used before being overwritten; flags self-assignments (x = x) and consecutive assignments to the same plain identifier with no intervening statement where the first value is immediately replaced

messages.redundantAssignment: This assignment is redundant; the value is never used before being overwritten.

rules.name: no-unused-collection
rules.description: Disallow const/let collection bindings (array, object, Map, Set, etc.) that are populated via mutating operations but whose contents are never read

messages.unusedCollection: The contents of this collection are never read; the collection is only ever written to.

rules.name: no-empty-collection
rules.description: Disallow reading from const/let array-like collection bindings (array, Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet) that are initialised empty and are never populated with any element

messages.emptyCollection: This collection is empty and is never populated; reading from it has no effect.

rules.name: no-redundant-parentheses
rules.description: Disallow redundant parentheses; flags only the unambiguous nested double-parenthesis subset ((x)) where the inner pair adds no grouping

rules.name: bool-param-default
rules.description: Require a default value for optional boolean function parameters typed exactly as the boolean keyword, so callers need not distinguish false from an omitted argument

messages.boolParamDefault: Provide a default value for this optional boolean parameter so callers are not forced to reason about three states.

rules.name: post-message
rules.description: Disallow sending cross-document messages with a "*" wildcard target origin; flags only the unambiguous postMessage(message, "*") sending subset

messages.postMessage: Specify a target origin instead of "*" for this cross-document message.

rules.name: in-operator-type-error
rules.description: Disallow the "in" operator with a primitive literal right operand (string, number, bigint, boolean, or null), which always throws a TypeError at runtime

messages.inOperatorTypeError: The right operand of "in" must be an object; a primitive value here throws a TypeError at runtime.

rules.name: different-types-comparison
rules.description: Disallow strict equality (===/!==) between two primitive literals of provably different types, where the comparison is always constant; flags only the unambiguous literal-vs-literal subset

messages.differentTypesComparison: These operands have different primitive types, so this strict comparison is always constant.

rules.name: operation-returning-nan
rules.description: Disallow arithmetic operations (excluding +) where an operand is a function, class, or plain object literal that always coerces to NaN

messages.operationReturningNan: This arithmetic operation will always evaluate to NaN.

rules.name: production-debug
rules.description: Disallow leaving debug features active in production code; flags only the unambiguous "debugger" statement (console.* and alert/confirm/prompt are not flagged)

rules.name: no-hardcoded-secrets
rules.description: Disallow hardcoded secret/credential string literals assigned to a secret-named identifier (secret, apiKey, token, access_token, private_key, etc.); provide secrets via configuration or environment variables instead

messages.hardcodedSecret: Revoke and change this secret, as it is compromised by being hardcoded here.

rules.name: concise-regex
rules.description: Prefer concise character class shorthands; flags only the exact verbose forms [0-9] (\d), [^0-9] (\D), and [A-Za-z0-9_] (\w)

messages.conciseRegex: Use a concise character class shorthand (\d, \D, or \w) instead of this verbose character class.

rules.name: no-misleading-character-class
rules.description: Disallow astral (multi-code-unit) characters in a regex character class without the "u"/"v" flag, where they are silently split into surrogate halves

messages.misleadingCharacterClass: This character class contains a multi-code-unit character; without the "u" flag it is split into surrogate halves and will not match as intended.

rules.name: slow-regex
rules.description: Disallow super-linear regular expressions; flags only the nested unbounded quantifier shape (e.g. (a+)+) that causes catastrophic backtracking

messages.slowRegex: This nested quantifier can cause catastrophic backtracking (super-linear runtime) on crafted input.

rules.name: web-sql-database
rules.description: Disallow use of the deprecated, removed, and security-sensitive Web SQL Database API; flags any call to openDatabase(...) (global or as a member, e.g. window.openDatabase)

messages.webSqlDatabase: The Web SQL Database API is deprecated and removed from the web platform; do not use it.

rules.name: no-intrusive-permissions
rules.description: Flag requests for intrusive browser permissions; matches the distinctive geolocation, Notification.requestPermission, and permissions.query call chains

messages.intrusivePermission: Requesting this browser permission is intrusive; make sure it is necessary and properly justified.

rules.name: encryption-secure-mode
rules.description: Disallow insecure block-cipher modes; flags Node crypto cipher/decipher factory calls whose string-literal cipher spec names the ECB or CBC mode (e.g. aes-128-cbc)

messages.insecureCipherMode: Use a secure cipher mode (e.g. GCM); ECB and CBC are vulnerable.

rules.name: no-unsafe-unzip
rules.description: Flag archive expansion that risks a zip-bomb denial of service; matches the distinctive adm-zip extractAllTo(...) method only (deliberately narrow to stay zero-false-positive)

messages.unsafeUnzip: Expanding this archive without limiting size or entry count risks a zip-bomb denial of service.

rules.name: disabled-timeout
rules.description: Disallow Mocha this.timeout() values that overflow the 32-bit setTimeout range (greater than 2147483647) and thereby silently disable the timeout instead of applying it

messages.disabledTimeout: This timeout value overflows the 32-bit range and silently disables the timeout; use 0 to disable intentionally or a value within range.

rules.name: cookie-no-httponly
rules.description: Flag cookie/session configuration that disables the HttpOnly flag; matches an object property "httpOnly" set to the boolean literal false

messages.cookieNoHttpOnly: Set this cookie's "httpOnly" flag to true to make it inaccessible to client-side scripts.

rules.name: content-security-policy
rules.description: Flag helmet configuration that disables the Content Security Policy; matches an object property "contentSecurityPolicy" set to the boolean literal false

messages.contentSecurityPolicy: Do not disable Content Security Policy (contentSecurityPolicy: false) — it is an important defense against XSS.

rules.name: certificate-transparency
rules.description: Flag helmet configuration that disables Certificate Transparency monitoring; matches an object property "expectCt" set to the boolean literal false

messages.certificateTransparency: Do not disable Certificate Transparency monitoring (expectCt: false).

rules.name: csrf
rules.description: Flag csurf middleware configuration that disables CSRF protection for unsafe HTTP methods; matches a csrf({ ignoreMethods: [...] }) call whose array lists a state-changing verb (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH)

messages.csrf: Do not disable CSRF protection for state-changing HTTP methods (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH).

rules.name: file-permissions
rules.description: Flag fs chmod-family calls or process.umask calls whose numeric mode grants permissions to "others"; only numeric-literal modes are checked (zero-false-positive)

messages.weakFilePermissions: Make sure this permissive file access is safe; granting access to "others" can be a security risk.

rules.name: file-uploads
rules.description: Flag multer.diskStorage configuration that omits an explicit destination; matches a diskStorage({ ... }) call whose object-literal first argument has no "destination" property (uploaded files would default to the OS temporary directory)

messages.fileUploads: Configure an explicit upload destination; without it, uploaded files are written to the OS temporary directory.

rules.name: cors
