rules.description: Flag permissive CORS configurations that trust any origin with the wildcard "*"; matches setHeader("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*"), cors({ origin: "*" }), and a headers object literal with Access-Control-Allow-Origin set to "*"

messages.cors: This permissive CORS policy allows any origin; restrict it to trusted origins.

rules.name: dns-prefetching
rules.description: Flag helmet configuration that re-enables DNS prefetching; matches a dnsPrefetchControl({ allow: true }) call whose allow property is the boolean literal true

messages.dnsPrefetching: Enabling DNS prefetching can leak information about the links a user is offered; disable it unless required.

rules.name: disabled-auto-escaping
rules.description: Flag disabling a template engine's HTML auto-escaping; matches an object property "noEscape" set to the boolean literal true (Handlebars) and assigning to Mustache.escape

messages.disabledAutoEscaping: Do not disable template auto-escaping; it is a key defense against XSS.

rules.name: aws-s3-bucket-granted-access
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK S3 bucket configured with an access control that grants access beyond the owner; matches an "accessControl" property whose value is a BucketAccessControl member named PUBLIC_READ, PUBLIC_READ_WRITE, or AUTHENTICATED_READ

messages.s3PublicAccess: This S3 bucket access control grants access beyond the owner; use a private access control.

rules.name: aws-rds-unencrypted-databases
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK RDS database or cluster created with encryption at rest disabled; matches an object property "storageEncrypted" set to the boolean literal false

messages.rdsUnencrypted: Enable encryption at rest for this database (storageEncrypted: true).

rules.name: aws-iam-public-access
rules.description: Flag AWS CDK IAM policies that grant public access to all AWS accounts; matches a new-expression whose callee is the AnyPrincipal class (a bare AnyPrincipal identifier or a member expression such as iam.AnyPrincipal)

messages.iamPublicAccess: This policy grants public access to all AWS accounts; restrict the principal.

rules.name: hidden-files
rules.description: Flag static file servers configured to serve dotfiles; matches an object property "dotfiles" set to the string literal "allow" (the distinctive serve-static / express.static option that exposes hidden files like .env or .git)

messages.hiddenFiles: Serving hidden files (dotfiles: 'allow') can expose sensitive files like .env or .git; use 'ignore' or 'deny'.

rules.name: aws-sqs-unencrypted-queue
rules.description: Flag AWS CDK SQS queues created with server-side encryption disabled; matches an object property "encryption" set to a member expression ending in UNENCRYPTED (QueueEncryption.UNENCRYPTED) or "sqsManagedSseEnabled" set to the boolean literal false

messages.sqsUnencrypted: Enable server-side encryption for this SQS queue.

rules.name: aws-apigateway-public-api
rules.description: Flag an AWS API Gateway method created without authorization; matches an object property "authorizationType" whose value is AuthorizationType.NONE (any member expression ending in NONE) or the string literal "NONE"

messages.apigatewayPublicApi: This API method has no authorization (authorizationType: NONE); require authorization or confirm public access is intended.

rules.name: aws-iam-all-privileges
rules.description: Flag AWS IAM policy statements that grant all privileges; matches an object property "actions" whose array literal value contains the wildcard string "*"

messages.iamAllPrivileges: This policy grants all actions ("*"); grant only the specific actions required.

rules.name: aws-s3-bucket-versioning
rules.description: Flag AWS CDK S3 bucket configuration that explicitly disables versioning; matches an object property "versioned" set to the boolean literal false (omission is deliberately not flagged to stay zero-false-positive)

messages.s3BucketVersioning: Versioning is disabled on this S3 bucket; enable it to protect against accidental or malicious data loss.

rules.name: aws-ec2-rds-dms-public
rules.description: Flag AWS resources (EC2 / RDS / DMS) made publicly accessible; matches an object property "publiclyAccessible" or "associatePublicIpAddress" whose value is the boolean literal true

messages.ec2RdsDmsPublic: Make sure allowing public network access to this resource is safe.

rules.name: aws-s3-bucket-public-access
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK S3 BlockPublicAccess configuration that disables public-access protection; matches an object property "blockPublicAcls", "blockPublicPolicy", "ignorePublicAcls", or "restrictPublicBuckets" whose value is the boolean literal false

messages.s3BucketPublicAccess: This S3 bucket does not block public access; ensure public exposure is intended.

rules.name: confidential-information-logging
rules.description: Flag a Signale logger configured without secret masking; matches a new-expression whose callee is Signale and whose first argument is an object literal with a "secrets" property set to an empty array literal

messages.confidentialLogging: Configure secret-masking patterns for this logger so confidential information is not logged.

rules.name: aws-iam-all-resources-accessible
rules.description: Flag AWS IAM policy statements that grant access to all resources; matches an object property "resources" whose array literal value contains the wildcard string "*"

messages.iamAllResources: This policy grants access to all resources ("*"); scope it to the specific resources required.

rules.name: aws-ec2-unencrypted-ebs-volume
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK EBS Volume created without encryption; matches a new-expression whose callee is the Volume construct and one of whose arguments is an object literal with an "encrypted" property set to the boolean literal false (the implicit/absent form is deliberately not flagged to stay zero-false-positive)

messages.ebsUnencrypted: Enable encryption for this EBS volume (encrypted: true).

rules.name: aws-efs-unencrypted
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK EFS FileSystem created without encryption at rest; matches a new-expression whose callee names the FileSystem construct and one of whose arguments is an object literal with an "encrypted" property set to the boolean literal false

messages.efsUnencrypted: Enable encryption at rest for this EFS file system (encrypted: true).

rules.name: aws-restricted-ip-admin-access
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK security group ingress rule that opens an administration port (SSH 22 or RDP 3389) to all IP addresses; matches an addIngressRule call whose peer argument is anyIpv4()/anyIpv6() and whose port argument is Port.tcp(22) or Port.tcp(3389)

messages.restrictedIpAdminAccess: Restrict access to administration ports (SSH/RDP) to specific trusted IP ranges instead of all addresses.

rules.name: redundant-type-aliases
rules.description: Flag a TypeScript type alias whose right-hand side merely renames an existing type; matches an alias with no type parameters whose declared type is a primitive/built-in keyword type or a bare type reference with no type arguments

messages.redundantTypeAlias: This type alias only renames an existing type and adds no value; use the underlying type directly.

rules.name: jsx-no-leaked-render
rules.description: Flag a logical-AND expression that conditionally renders JSX where the left operand is numeric (a `.length` member access or a numeric literal), e.g. `{items.length && <List/>}`; a falsy numeric `0` is rendered as the text "0" instead of nothing. Boolean comparisons (`> 0`, `!== 0`), plain identifiers, non-`length` members, the `||` operator and non-JSX right operands are not flagged to stay zero-false-positive

messages.jsxNoLeakedRender: Convert this `&&` to a boolean condition (e.g. `x.length > 0`); a numeric left operand can leak 0 into the rendered output.

rules.name: no-uniq-key
rules.description: Flag a JSX key attribute whose value is a Math.random() or Date.now() call; such a value differs on every render, so React keys never match up between renders and the DOM is needlessly recreated (zero-false-positive syntactic subset of S6486)

messages.noUniqKey: Do not use a random or time-based value as a React key; it changes every render and defeats reconciliation.

rules.name: insecure-cookie
rules.description: Flag a cookie configuration object that sets "secure" to the boolean literal false, which lets the cookie be sent over unencrypted HTTP; gated to stay zero-false-positive by requiring a distinctive cookie-marker sibling key (httpOnly, sameSite, maxAge, domain, path, or signed) in the same object literal

messages.insecureCookie: Set this cookie's "secure" flag to true so it is only sent over HTTPS.

rules.name: no-hook-setter-in-body
rules.description: Flag a React useState setter that is called directly in a component body (a direct top-level expression statement of the same function), which schedules a state update on every render and causes an infinite re-render loop; function-local detection scans only the direct statements of one body, so calls inside handlers, effects, callbacks, conditionals, loops, or JSX are not flagged (zero-false-positive subset of S6442)

messages.noHookSetterInBody: Do not call a useState setter directly in the component body; it triggers an infinite re-render. Move it into an event handler or effect.

rules.name: content-length
rules.description: Flag an upload-size limit larger than 8MB, expressed as a fileSize/maxFileSize object property or a .fileSize/.maxFileSize member assignment whose value is a numeric literal greater than 8000000; an excessive limit enables denial-of-service attacks (zero-false-positive subset of S5693 that skips string limits and generic keys)

messages.contentLength: This file-size limit is very large; cap upload size to mitigate denial-of-service risk.

rules.name: unverified-certificate
rules.description: Flag a rejectUnauthorized object property whose value is the boolean literal false, which disables TLS server-certificate validation in Node.js https/tls/request options and exposes the connection to man-in-the-middle attacks; only the distinctive rejectUnauthorized key with a literal false value is reported (zero-false-positive subset of S4830)

messages.unverifiedCertificate: Enable server certificate validation (do not set rejectUnauthorized to false).

rules.name: no-mime-sniff
rules.description: Flag an object property whose key is exactly "noSniff" set to the boolean literal false, which disables helmet's X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff protection and exposes the app to MIME confusion attacks; the distinctive helmet key makes this a zero-false-positive subset of S5734

messages.noMimeSniff: Do not disable the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff protection (noSniff: false).

rules.name: no-ip-forward
rules.description: Flag an http-proxy / http-proxy-middleware configuration that enables client-IP forwarding, expressed as an object property whose key is exactly xfwd and whose value is the boolean literal true; forwarding the client IP can leak personal information and enable IP-based access-control bypass (zero-false-positive subset of S5759 keyed on the distinctive xfwd option)

messages.noIpForward: Forwarding the client IP (xfwd: true) can enable IP-based access-control bypass; ensure this is safe.

rules.name: no-angular-bypass-sanitization
rules.description: Flag calls to Angular DomSanitizer bypassSecurityTrust* methods (bypassSecurityTrustHtml, bypassSecurityTrustStyle, bypassSecurityTrustScript, bypassSecurityTrustUrl, bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl), which disable Angular's built-in XSS sanitization; the method names are essentially unique to DomSanitizer so any such call is reported (S6268)

messages.angularBypassSanitization: Bypassing Angular's built-in sanitization is security-sensitive; ensure the value is trusted.

rules.name: insecure-jwt-token
rules.description: Flag a JWT options object property that disables signature protection: a key of algorithm whose value is the string literal 'none', or a key of algorithms whose array value contains the string literal 'none' (both compared case-insensitively); the 'none' JWT algorithm disables signature verification and lets tokens be forged (zero-false-positive subset of S5659 keyed on the distinctive algorithm/algorithms options)

messages.insecureJwtToken: Do not use the "none" algorithm for JWTs; it disables signature verification.

rules.name: xml-parser-xxe
rules.description: Flag an object property whose key is the boolean option noent and whose value is the boolean literal true; libxmljs parseXmlString enables external entity expansion when noent: true is set, exposing the application to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks (zero-false-positive subset of S2755 keyed on the distinctive noent option)

messages.xmlParserXxe: Disable external entity expansion (do not set noent: true) to prevent XXE attacks.

rules.name: no-useless-react-setstate
rules.description: Flag a React useState setter called with its own paired state variable (setV(v) where const [v, setV] = useState(...)); React bails out of the re-render because the next value is identical, so the call is a no-op. The state binding is const and the pair is collected from the same destructuring, making an exact setter(state) match zero-false-positive (S6443)

messages.noUselessReactSetstate: This setState call passes the current state value and has no effect.

rules.name: no-referrer-policy
rules.description: Flag a helmet referrerPolicy option set to a leaky Referrer-Policy value ('no-referrer-when-downgrade' or 'unsafe-url'), which sends the full URL to other origins and can expose confidential data; flags a policy property whose string value is one of those distinctive tokens (zero-false-positive subset of S5736)

messages.noReferrerPolicy: This Referrer-Policy value leaks the full URL to other origins; use a stricter policy like 'no-referrer' or 'same-origin'.

rules.name: weak-ssl
rules.description: Flag a TLS/SSL options object configured with a weak, deprecated protocol version: a secureProtocol string of 'TLSv1_method', 'TLSv1_1_method', 'SSLv2_method', 'SSLv3_method', or 'SSLv23_method', or a minVersion/maxVersion string of 'TLSv1' or 'TLSv1.1'. Those distinctive Node.js TLS constants make this a zero-false-positive subset of S4423; use TLS 1.2 or higher instead

messages.weakSsl: Use a strong TLS protocol version (TLS 1.2 or higher); this configures a weak/deprecated protocol.

rules.name: no-weak-keys
rules.description: Flag a crypto.generateKeyPair/generateKeyPairSync call that generates a weak asymmetric key: an options object with a modulusLength numeric literal below 2048 (RSA/DSA/DH) or a namedCurve string literal naming a curve below 224 bits; the distinctive generateKeyPair* callee plus a literal weak parameter keeps this a zero-false-positive subset of S4426

messages.weakKeys: Use a strong key size (RSA/DSA/DH >= 2048 bits, or a strong EC curve).

rules.name: strict-transport-security
rules.description: Flag a weak helmet hsts configuration: an hsts call whose first object argument either disables includeSubDomains (set to false) or sets maxAge to a numeric literal below the recommended six-month minimum of 15552000 seconds; the distinctive hsts method name keeps this zero-false-positive (S5739)

messages.strictTransportSecurity: Strengthen this HSTS policy (enable includeSubDomains and use a long max-age).

rules.name: unverified-hostname
rules.description: Flag a checkServerIdentity option whose value is a function literal with a trivial always-pass body (an empty block, a bare return / return true / return undefined, or an arrow expression body of true), which disables TLS hostname verification; the distinctive key plus empty override is a zero-false-positive subset of S5527

messages.unverifiedHostname: This checkServerIdentity override disables TLS hostname verification; validate the server hostname.

rules.name: frame-ancestors
rules.description: Flag a helmet contentSecurityPolicy frameAncestors directive set to the array value "'none'" (the documented Noncompliant pattern of S5732); the distinctive frameAncestors key plus the CSP 'none' keyword keeps this a zero-false-positive subset

messages.frameAncestors: Make sure this Content Security Policy frame-ancestors directive is safe here.

rules.name: no-table-as-layout
rules.description: Flag a JSX <table> element carrying role="presentation" or role="none" (the documented Noncompliant pattern of S5257), which marks a layout table that confuses screen readers; matching this exact shape keeps it zero-false-positive

messages.noTableAsLayout: Do not use an HTML <table> for layout; a table with role="presentation"/"none" confuses screen readers. Use CSS instead.

rules.name: no-vue-bypass-sanitization
rules.description: Flag the Vue.js raw-HTML render patterns of S6299: a JSX domPropsInnerHTML attribute, or a domProps object literal containing an innerHTML property; both bypass Vue built-in escaping. The distinctive domProps/domPropsInnerHTML shapes keep this zero-false-positive (v-html templates are out of scope)

messages.noVueBypassSanitization: Make sure disabling Vue.js built-in escaping (rendering raw HTML) is safe here.

rules.name: os-command
rules.description: Flag a child_process spawn/spawnSync/execFile/execFileSync call passed an options object with shell:true (the shell-interpreter form of S4721), which risks OS command injection; exec/execSync are intentionally excluded to avoid colliding with RegExp.exec, keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.osCommand: Make sure using a shell to execute this OS command is safe here.

rules.name: argument-type
rules.description: Flag a single-argument Math.* numeric call (abs, floor, sqrt, ...) whose argument is a boolean-producing expression — a comparison or a logical-not — which is the documented type-mismatch bug of S3782 (e.g. Math.abs(x < 0.0042)); logical &&/|| arguments are not flagged because they evaluate to an operand (often a number), so requiring a boolean-producing argument keeps this zero-false-positive

messages.argumentType: Pass a number to this Math function; this argument evaluates to a boolean.

rules.name: aws-s3-bucket-insecure-http
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK S3 bucket property enforceSSL:false (the explicit insecure-HTTP form of S6249), which authorizes cleartext HTTP access. The distinctive enforceSSL key keeps this zero-false-positive; omission is intentionally not flagged

messages.s3BucketInsecureHttp: Enforce HTTPS-only access on this S3 bucket (set enforceSSL: true) instead of allowing insecure HTTP.

rules.name: aws-s3-bucket-server-encryption
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK S3 bucket encryption property set to BucketEncryption.UNENCRYPTED (or the string "UNENCRYPTED") — the explicit disable-encryption form of S6245. The distinctive encryption key plus UNENCRYPTED value keeps this zero-false-positive; omission is intentionally not flagged

messages.s3BucketServerEncryption: Do not disable server-side encryption on this S3 bucket; use a managed or KMS encryption mode instead of UNENCRYPTED.

rules.name: aws-opensearchservice-domain
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK OpenSearch domain whose encryptionAtRest or encryptionAtRestOptions sets enabled:false — the explicit disable-encryption form of S6308. The distinctive nested shape keeps this zero-false-positive; omission is intentionally not flagged

messages.opensearchUnencrypted: Enable encryption at rest on this OpenSearch/Elasticsearch domain (set enabled: true).

rules.name: cookies
rules.description: Flag cookie-write operations (the security hotspot S2255): a document.cookie assignment, a .cookie(name, value) call, or a setHeader("Set-Cookie", ...) call. The distinctive cookie-write shapes keep this zero-false-positive; cookie reads are not flagged

messages.cookies: Make sure writing this cookie is safe; cookies are client-accessible and should not carry sensitive data.

rules.name: xpath
rules.description: Flag XPath-execution calls (the security hotspot S4817): document.evaluate(...), .selectNodes(...), .SelectSingleNode(...), or xpath.select/select1(...). Generic method names are receiver-gated (document/xpath) to keep this zero-false-positive

messages.xpath: Make sure executing this XPath expression is safe; validate or sanitize any user-controlled input.

rules.name: sockets
rules.description: Flag raw socket usage on the Node net module (the security hotspot S4818): new net.Socket(), net.createConnection(...), or net.connect(...). Gating on the net receiver keeps the generic connect/createConnection names zero-false-positive

messages.sockets: Make sure that using this socket is safe here.

rules.name: existing-groups
rules.description: Flag a String.replace/replaceAll call whose literal replacement string references a regex group that does not exist (S6328): a $<name> with no such named group, $0, or a $N greater than the capturing-group count. Only literal regex + literal replacement are analyzed, keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.existingGroups: This replacement references a regular expression group that does not exist; use a valid group reference.

rules.name: encryption
rules.description: Flag data-encryption calls (the security hotspot S4787): the Node crypto createCipher/createCipheriv/createDecipher/createDecipheriv/publicEncrypt/privateDecrypt/privateEncrypt/publicDecrypt functions, and crypto.subtle.encrypt/decrypt. Generic encrypt/decrypt are gated on a .subtle receiver to keep this zero-false-positive

messages.encryption: Make sure that encrypting data is safe here (review the algorithm, key, IV, and padding).

rules.name: regular-expr
rules.description: Flag a hardcoded regular expression with at least 3 characters and at least 2 of the characters *, +, or { (the documented heuristic of the security hotspot S4784): a regex literal, new RegExp("..."), or a .search/.match("...") string argument. Deprecated upstream in favor of slow-regex (S5852)

messages.regularExpr: Make sure that using this hardcoded regular expression is safe here.

rules.name: no-os-command-from-path
rules.description: Flag a child_process spawn/spawnSync/execFile/execFileSync call whose first argument is a bare command name string literal (no path separator), which resolves via PATH (the security hotspot S4036). exec/execSync are excluded to avoid colliding with RegExp.exec, keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.noOsCommandFromPath: Use a fully-qualified absolute path for this OS command instead of relying on the PATH environment variable.

rules.name: publicly-writable-directories
rules.description: Flag a hardcoded publicly-writable directory (the security hotspot S5443): a string literal under /tmp, /var/tmp, /usr/tmp or /dev/shm, or a process.env.TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP access. The distinctive temp-directory paths keep this zero-false-positive

messages.publiclyWritableDirectories: Make sure using this publicly writable directory is safe here.

rules.name: chai-determinate-assertion
rules.description: Flag non-deterministic Chai assertions in an expect(...) chain (S6092): negated .throw(arg)/.include(obj)/.property(2+)/.ownPropertyDescriptor(2+)/.members/.increase/.decrease/.by, the .not.finite property, and .change(...).by(...). Gated on an expect-rooted chain to stay zero-false-positive

messages.chaiDeterminateAssertion: This Chai assertion can succeed for more than one reason; make it assert a single deterministic condition.

rules.name: no-async-constructor
rules.description: Flag a class constructor whose top-level body starts an asynchronous operation (a .then/.catch/.finally call or a Promise.resolve/all/... call), per S7059. Only direct statements are inspected (nested callbacks are not), keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.noAsyncConstructor: Do not start asynchronous operations in a constructor; move the async work to an async method or a static factory method.

rules.name: unused-import
rules.description: Flag an import specifier whose binding is never referenced in the module (S1128), using semantic scope analysis; side-effect imports (import "x") are never flagged

rules.name: aws-sagemaker-unencrypted-notebook
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK CfnNotebookInstance constructed without a kmsKeyId property (the security hotspot S6319), leaving the notebook unencrypted. Gated on the distinctive CfnNotebookInstance construct name to stay zero-false-positive

messages.sagemakerUnencryptedNotebook: Set a KMS key (kmsKeyId) on this SageMaker notebook instance to encrypt its data at rest.

rules.name: aws-sns-unencrypted-topics
rules.description: Flag an AWS CDK SNS topic constructed without encryption (the security hotspot S6327): a CfnTopic without kmsMasterKeyId, or an sns.Topic without masterKey. Gated on the distinctive CfnTopic name and the sns.Topic receiver to stay zero-false-positive (a bare new Topic() is not flagged)

messages.snsUnencryptedTopic: Encrypt this SNS topic by setting a KMS master key.

rules.name: no-globals-shadowing
rules.description: Flag binding or assigning the special identifiers eval, arguments, undefined, NaN, or Infinity (S2137). Reads are not flagged; only bind/assign/update targets are. Matching this fixed name set keeps it zero-false-positive

messages.noGlobalsShadowing: Do not bind or assign this special identifier; rename it.

rules.name: no-unused-vars
rules.description: Flag a function-local variable declared with a plain identifier binding that is never read (S1481), using semantic analysis. Top-level/exported declarations, destructuring patterns, and underscore-prefixed names are deliberately not flagged to stay zero-false-positive

rules.name: strings-comparison
rules.description: Flag a relational comparison (</>/<=/>=) where both operands are string literals (S3003), which performs a surprising lexicographic comparison. Only the literal form is flagged (the typed-variable form needs type inference), keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.stringsComparison: Do not compare strings with </>/<=/>=; this is a lexicographic comparison. Convert to numbers (Number(...)) if a numeric comparison is intended.

rules.name: non-number-in-arithmetic-expression
rules.description: Flag an arithmetic operator (- * / % **) with a string-literal or boolean-literal operand (S3760), which relies on implicit type conversion. Binary + (concatenation) is excluded; only the literal form is flagged (the typed-variable form needs type inference), keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.nonNumberInArithmetic: Use only numeric operands with this arithmetic operator; a string or boolean operand relies on implicit conversion.

rules.name: useless-string-operation
rules.description: Flag a statement-level call to a string-specific immutable method (toUpperCase, trim, padStart, ...) whose returned value is discarded (S1154). Methods shared with arrays or commonly user-defined are excluded to stay zero-false-positive

messages.uselessStringOperation: The result of this string operation is ignored; strings are immutable, so assign or use the returned value.

rules.name: no-incorrect-string-concat
rules.description: Flag a + expression concatenating a string literal with an object, array, or function literal (S3402) — an unambiguous coercion bug. The general string+number/variable case needs type inference and is deliberately not flagged, keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.incorrectStringConcat: Do not concatenate a string with an object, array, or function; this relies on an unintended string coercion.

rules.name: table-header
rules.description: Flag a static JSX <table> that contains <td> cells but no <th> header cell (S5256). Tables with any dynamic {…} content are skipped to stay zero-false-positive

messages.tableHeader: Add a <th> header cell to this table so assistive technologies can describe its columns/rows.

rules.name: table-header-reference
rules.description: Flag a static JSX table cell whose headers attribute references an id that is absent from the table (S5260). Tables with dynamic {…} content or non-literal id/headers values are skipped, and the row/column-correctness check is out of scope, to stay zero-false-positive

messages.tableHeaderReference: This headers attribute references a header id that does not exist in the table.

rules.name: no-return-type-any
rules.description: Flag a function or method whose explicit return type annotation is the bare any type (S4324), which discards type safety for callers. Composite types like any[] or Promise<any> and functions without a return annotation are not flagged, keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.noReturnTypeAny: Use a more specific return type than any.

rules.name: values-not-convertible-to-numbers
rules.description: Flag a relational comparison (< > <= >=) where an operand is an object, function, or arrow literal (S3758) — it coerces to NaN, so the comparison is always false. Arrays/strings/variables are excluded (coercion ambiguity, other rules, or type inference needed), keeping this zero-false-positive

messages.valuesNotConvertibleToNumbers: This operand does not convert to a number, so the comparison is always false.

rules.name: arrow-function-convention
rules.description: Enforce consistent as-needed use of parentheses around a single arrow-function parameter and braces around a single-expression arrow body.

messages.removeParens: Remove the parentheses around this single arrow-function parameter.
messages.removeBraces: Remove the curly braces and 'return' keyword from this arrow-function body.

rules.name: assertions-in-tests
rules.description: Tests should include assertions; a test case with no assertion can only fail by throwing and gives a false sense of safety.

messages.addAssertion: Add at least one assertion to this test case.

rules.name: aws-iam-privilege-escalation
rules.description: AWS IAM policies that grant sensitive IAM/STS actions on all resources enable privilege escalation and should restrict the resource scope.

messages.iamPrivilegeEscalation: Narrow the resource scope of this policy: granting this sensitive IAM action on all resources ("*") enables privilege escalation.

rules.name: comment-regex
rules.description: Flag every comment whose text matches a configured regular expression (the "regularExpression" option); the pattern is compiled once per file and tested unanchored against each comment including its delimiters. Empty pattern disables the rule; the core narrow default is the literal marker XXX.

messages.commentRegex: This comment matches the configured comment-regex pattern; review or remove it.

rules.name: conditional-indentation
rules.description: An unbraced statement controlled by an if/for/while on the line below should be indented further than its control keyword.

messages.conditionalIndentation: Use indentation to denote the code that is conditionally executed by this control statement.

rules.name: disabled-resource-integrity
rules.description: External (cross-origin) script and stylesheet resources should be loaded with a Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash via the integrity attribute.

messages.disabledResourceIntegrity: Make sure not using resource integrity feature is safe here. Add an "integrity" attribute to verify this cross-origin resource.

rules.name: dompurify-unsafe-config
rules.description: Disallow DOMPurify configuration options that bypass sanitization (e.g. ALLOW_UNKNOWN_PROTOCOLS: true).

messages.unsafeConfig: Setting "ALLOW_UNKNOWN_PROTOCOLS" to true lets unsafe URI protocols bypass DOMPurify sanitization. Remove this option or set it to false.

rules.name: dynamically-constructed-templates
rules.description: Angular templates should not be constructed dynamically, which bypasses the framework's contextual escaping and risks template injection.

messages.dynamicTemplate: Refrain from constructing this Angular template dynamically; build a static template and pass runtime data through bindings to avoid template injection.

rules.name: file-header
rules.description: Require every file to start with a configured header banner (the "headerFormat" option). Inactive when no header is configured (the SonarJS default). Line endings are normalized; the regular-expression "isRegularExpression" mode is not supported in this port and is skipped.

messages.fileHeader: Add or update the header of this file so it matches the required header.

rules.name: function-return-type
rules.description: Functions should always return the same type.

messages.differentTypes: Refactor this function to always return the same type.

rules.name: hardcoded-secret-signatures
rules.description: Hard-coded secrets matching a well-known credential signature should be revoked and removed from source.

messages.hardcodedSecret: Revoke and change this leaked secret, then remove the hard-coded value from the source code.

rules.name: no-implicit-global
rules.description: Disallow accidental creation of global variables by assigning to an undeclared name.

messages.implicitGlobal: Add the "let", "const" or "var" keyword to "{{value}}" so it is not implicitly created in the global scope.

rules.name: no-internal-api-use
rules.description: Internal framework APIs (such as React internals named with the DO_NOT_USE token) should not be used.

messages.noInternalApiUse: Remove this usage of an internal framework API; it is not part of the public contract and may change or break without notice.

rules.name: no-mixed-content
rules.description: Content Security Policy directives should block mixed content by including the block-all-mixed-content directive.

messages.addBlockAllMixedContent: Make sure allowing mixed-content is safe here; add the "block-all-mixed-content" directive to this Content Security Policy.

rules.name: no-selector-parameter
rules.description: Boolean selector parameters used only to branch behaviour inside a function should be replaced by separate, clearly named functions.

messages.selectorParameter: Provide multiple methods instead of using this selector parameter.

rules.name: no-session-cookies-on-static-assets
rules.description: Session middleware should not run before static-asset handlers, otherwise static assets needlessly receive a session cookie.

messages.noSessionCookiesOnStaticAssets: Serve static assets before registering the session middleware so static-asset responses do not set a session cookie.

rules.name: no-try-promise
rules.description: Promises produced inside a try block must be awaited, otherwise their asynchronous rejection escapes the catch handler.

messages.tryPromise: Consider awaiting this promise inside the 'try', or handle its rejection with '.catch()'; a 'try'/'catch' will not intercept the rejection of an unawaited promise.

rules.name: null-dereference
rules.description: Properties of variables with a "null" or "undefined" value should not be accessed.

messages.nullDereference: This expression dereferences "null" or "undefined" and will throw a TypeError at runtime.

rules.name: prefer-read-only-props
rules.description: React function component props should be declared with a read-only type.

messages.readonlyProps: Mark the props of the component as read-only.

rules.name: prefer-type-guard
rules.description: A function that returns the result of a single `instanceof` check on its parameter should declare a type-predicate return type so callers can narrow the value's type.

messages.preferTypeGuard: Declare this function's return type using a type predicate (e.g. `arg is SomeType`) so callers can narrow the argument's type.

rules.name: regex-complexity
rules.description: Regular expressions should not be too complicated.

messages.complexity: Simplify this regular expression; its structural complexity of {{value}} exceeds the maximum allowed.

rules.name: review-blockchain-mnemonic
rules.description: Blockchain wallet mnemonic (seed) phrases should not be hardcoded or disclosed.

messages.reviewMnemonic: Make sure this blockchain wallet mnemonic phrase is not disclosed.

rules.name: session-regeneration
rules.description: A new session should be created during user authentication; regenerate the session after Passport authentication to prevent session fixation.

messages.createSession: Create a new session during user authentication to prevent session fixation attacks.

rules.name: sql-queries
rules.description: Formatting SQL queries by mixing runtime values into the statement text (string concatenation or template interpolation) is security-sensitive and a common source of SQL injection.

messages.safeQuery: Make sure that formatting this SQL query is safe here.

rules.name: stable-tests
rules.description: Tests should be stable: flags automatic retry of flaky tests via jest.retryTimes(n) or Mocha this.retries(n) with a positive count.

messages.stableTests: Make your tests stable so that they pass on the first try, or remove this retry configuration.

rules.name: stateful-regex
rules.description: Regular expressions stored in a variable with the global or sticky flag are stateful and produce surprising results when reused with test()/exec().

messages.statefulRegex: This stateful regular expression keeps its global ('g') or sticky ('y') match position between calls; reusing it via test()/exec() can yield inconsistent results. Reset lastIndex, drop the flag, or create a fresh regex per call.

rules.name: test-check-exception
rules.description: Tests that wrap throwing code in try/catch must assert that an exception was actually thrown; otherwise the test passes silently when nothing throws.

messages.checkException: Add an assertion that forces this test to fail when no exception is thrown.

rules.name: unused-named-groups
rules.description: Named capturing groups in a regular expression should be referenced; flags named groups that are never used.

messages.unusedNamedGroups: Use the named group '{{value}}' of this regex or remove the name as it is never referenced.

rules.name: variable-name
rules.description: Local variable and function parameter names should comply with a naming convention (default camelCase: ^[_a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$).

messages.renameVariable: Rename this local variable or parameter "{{value}}" to match the regular expression {{format}}.

rules.name: x-powered-by
rules.description: Disclosing the technology stack via the X-Powered-By header is security-sensitive; explicitly enabling it is flagged.

messages.xPoweredBy: Make sure disclosing the fingerprint of this web technology is safe here; disable the "x-powered-by" header instead.

rules.name: eol-last

rules.name: no-trailing-spaces

rules.name: template-curly-spacing

rules.name: rest-spread-spacing

rules.name: no-whitespace-before-property

rules.name: dot-location

messages.expectedDotAfterObject: Expected dot to be on same line as object.
messages.expectedDotBeforeProperty: Expected dot to be on same line as property.

rules.name: spaced-comment
rules.description: Enforce consistent spacing after the // or /* in a comment.

rules.name: object-curly-spacing
rules.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside braces of object literals, destructuring, and imports/exports.

messages.unexpectedSpaceAfter: There should be no space after '{'.
messages.unexpectedSpaceBefore: There should be no space before '}'.

rules.name: array-bracket-spacing
rules.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside array brackets.

messages.unexpectedSpaceAfter: There should be no space after '['.

rules.name: computed-property-spacing

messages.unexpectedSpaceAfter: There should be no space after '['.

rules.name: block-spacing
rules.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside single-line blocks.

messages.extra: There should be no space inside of braces.
messages.missing: Requires a space inside of braces.

rules.description: Require or disallow padding within blocks.

rules.name: space-before-blocks

rules.name: space-before-function-paren
